My COMP110 Survey Analysis: LSQS, Programming and Understanding

1. Project Summary

Practice is directly proportional to understanding. We recommend first piloting the addition of optional practice questions and then deciding whether to roll them out fully based on completion rates and student feedback.

2. Data Visualizations

<img width="875" height="836" alt="截屏2026-04-26 21 07 40" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b3bd1585-58ac-49cf-b641-e720db65b432" />

3. Conclusion

The data shows that nearly 70% of students recognize the value of practice, and that the effectiveness of practice is positively correlated with the level of understanding, indicating that the approach of “increasing the number of practice questions” is reasonable. In particular, for the small proportion of students with lower levels of understanding (scoring 1–2 points), additional practice may be the help they need most. Therefore, I recommend adding optional, ungraded practice questions accompanied by answer explanations, allowing students who need them to practice on their own without increasing the burden on others. Of course, this approach comes with costs: teachers and students will need additional time to create questions and provide feedback, and some students may feel increased pressure. Furthermore, the current analysis relies primarily on indirect data and cannot directly prove that students actually want more practice. I suggest including a direct scale question in the next survey: “More ungraded practice questions would help me learn” (rated 1–7), to gain a more accurate understanding of students’ genuine needs.
